target.name: Ulysses Robotics
target.founder: Akhil Voorakkara, Jamie Wedderburn, Colm O'Brien, Will O'Brien

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $38M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Ulysses Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $38M to date. The valuation analysis employs a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable across the robotics and manufacturing sector, primarily at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $38M total raised as a proxy valuation base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a valuation of $76M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (1X Technologies at $10.0B, Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B, Dexterity at $1.6B, Collaborative Robotics at $500M, and Fairmat at $350M, among others) yielding a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated valuation range is $65M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $576M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in comparable selection and the valuation heterogeneity within frontier robotics, where stage-progression and peer-median methods diverge substantially. The peer-median skews upward due to inclusion of later-stage and later-funded peers; a Series A-specific peer refinement would likely compress the range. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, path to commercialization, and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort.
